content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
James R. Mills 
277 Sea Forest Court 
Del Mar, CA 
92014 
Dear Mr. Mills: 
September 25, 1986 
Re: 
86-280 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on September 22, 1986 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days. 
JGM:plh 
vttl:u~ .7!C-'~ 
J~n G. McLean 
Counsel 
Legal Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322,5660

r 
September 17, 1986 
,\~ 
~ ~S~" '. 
, 
Dan Stanford, Chair 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K Street 
Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Dear Mr. Stanford: 
277 Sea Forest Court 
Del Mar, CA 
92014 
I expect to be engaged as a consultant by a campaign committee 
organized to seek the passage of a measure on the ballot this 
coming fall. 
It will be presented to the voters of San Diego 
County. 
If I am engaged by that campaign committee, am I prohibited from 
contributing from my personal campaign funds to the passage of 
the proposition? 
I do feel strongly about the proposition and would like to see it 
passed. 
I would like to be able to contribute money to its 
passage, but not if you would find that action in conflict with 
the law, obviously. 
S11' . cerelY/,!' 
/. 
t\ 
/j 
. 
' 
i 
/' 
'. 
15 V 
/ . 
JAMES R. MILLS 
Former Senator, 40th District 
JRM:chh 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
James R. Mills 
Former Senator, 40th District 
277 Sea Forest Court 
Del Mar, CA 
92014 
Dear Mr. Mills: 
October 10, 1986 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our Advice No. A-86-280 
Thank you for your request for advice regarding your duties 
under the Political Reform Act.!! 
You have asked if you would 
be prohibited from contributing your leftover campaign funds to 
a committee organized to seek the passage of a measure on the 
local ballot this coming fall, by virtue of the fact that you 
expect to be engaged as a consultant by the committee. 
While the Act deals with the campaign reporting requirements 
surrounding such a transaction, the provisions of law which deal 
with personal use of campaign funds (Elections Code section 
12400, et seg.) are not under the jurisdiction of the 
Commission. 
Therefore, we must recommend that you contact the 
Attorney General's office for advice regarding these provisions 
of law. 
In addition, San Diego County has a local campaign 
ordinance which may be of importance. 
I have asked the 
San Diego County Registrar of voters to send you a copy of the 
ordinance. 
Of course, that ordinance is interpreted by the 
County of San Diego. 
If you should have any questions, please contact me at (916) 
322-5901. 
JGM:km 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths

sections.question: If I am engaged as a consultant by a campaign committee organized to seek the passage of a measure on the ballot this coming fall to be presented to the voters of San Diego County, am I prohibited from contributing from my personal campaign funds to the passage of the proposition?
sections.conclusion: While the Act deals with the campaign reporting requirements surrounding such a transaction, the provisions of law which deal with personal use of campaign funds (Elections Code section 12400, et seq.) are not under the jurisdiction of the Commission. Therefore, we must recommend that you contact the Attorney General's office for advice regarding these provisions of law. In addition, San Diego County has a local campaign ordinance which may be of importance.
sections.question_synthetic: Mr. Mills asks whether, as a consultant to a ballot measure campaign committee, he is prohibited from contributing his leftover personal campaign funds to that same committee.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC lacks jurisdiction over personal use of campaign funds and cannot provide definitive advice on this matter. The requestor should contact the California Attorney General's office regarding Elections Code section 12400 et seq., and should also consult San Diego County's local campaign ordinance.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a complete advice letter with both the incoming request (dated September 17, 1986) and the FPPC response (dated October 10, 1986). The conclusion states the FPPC's declination of jurisdiction rather than providing substantive legal advice. Document is clearly structured with question and conclusion sections.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: If I am engaged as a consultant by a campaign committee organized to seek the passage of a measure on the ballot this coming fall to be presented to the voters of San Diego County, am I prohibited from contributing from my personal campaign funds to the passage of the proposition?

CONCLUSION: While the Act deals with the campaign reporting requirements surrounding such a transaction, the provisions of law which deal with personal use of campaign funds (Elections Code section 12400, et seq.) are not under the jurisdiction of the Commission. Therefore, we must recommend that you contact the Attorney General's office for advice regarding these provisions of law. In addition, San Diego County has a local campaign ordinance which may be of importance.

If I am engaged by that campaign committee, am I prohibited from contributing from my personal campaign funds to the passage of the proposition? MILLS Former Senator, 40th District JRM:chh California Fair Political Practices Commission James R. Mills Former Senator, 40th District 277 Sea Forest Court Del Mar, CA 92014 Dear Mr. Mills: October 10, 1986 Re: Your Request for Advice Our Advice No. A-86-280 Thank you for your request for advice regarding your duties under the Political Reform Act.!! You have asked if you would be prohibited from contributing your leftover campaign funds to a committee organized to seek the passage of a measure on the local ballot this coming fall, by virtue of the fact that you expect to be engaged as a consultant by the committee. While the Act deals with the campaign reporting requirements surrounding such a transaction, the provisions of law which deal with personal use of campaign funds (Elections Code section 12400, et seg.) are not under the jurisdiction of the Commission. Therefore, we must recommend that you contact the Attorney General's office for advice regarding these provisions of law. In addition, San Diego County has a local campaign ordinance which may be of importance. I have asked the San Diego County Registrar of voters to send you a copy of the ordinance. Of course, that ordinance is interpreted by the County of San Diego. If you should have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5901. JGM:km Sincerely,
embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter 86-280 addresses whether a former state senator engaged as a consultant to a ballot measure campaign can contribute personal campaign funds to that campaign. The FPPC declined jurisdiction, directing the requestor to the Attorney General's office and local county ordinances for guidance.
